briefing.community_pulse:
• Mira Murati's Thinking Machines announces 1GW Nvidia Vera Rubin compute partnership, generating significant buzz as one of the biggest ex-OpenAI founder moves yet — her quote 'bringing adaptable collaborative AI to everyone' is circulating widely
• Fei-Fei Li publishes spatial intelligence manifesto on Substack, featured on a16z, arguing 'spatial intelligence will transform how we create and interact with real and virtual worlds — revolutionizing storytelling, creativity, robotics, scientific discovery'
• Ethan Mollick's 'Three Years from GPT-3 to Gemini 3' post goes wide: he prompted Gemini 3 to demonstrate progress and it built a fully playable 'Candy-Powered FTL Starship Simulator' — used as a vivid proxy for how far AI has come
• Jensen Huang's late-March Lex Fridman claim 'I think we've achieved AGI' continues to ripple through AI Twitter, fueling AGI timeline debate and pushback on definitional goalpost-shifting
• Vibe coding backlash intensifies: practitioners calling out AI coding agents like SWE-agent for enabling tech debt when used without rigorous review — 'beyond the vibes' framing gaining traction in dev communities
• Agent framework wars heat up: LangGraph vs. CrewAI vs. AutoGen comparisons dominate developer LinkedIn and dev.to, with convergence toward graph-based state machines seen as the mature path
• ElevenLabs Eleven v3 launch driving excitement for real-time voice AI agents with emotional expressiveness (sighing, whispering, laughing) at under 150ms latency for STT — practitioners flagging it as a step-change for voice products
• Benchmark trust crisis deepens: SWE-bench contamination and GitHub data leakage debates resurface, with calls to retire MMLU and GPQA as meaningful signals — 'benchmarks are not trustworthy' framing going viral
• Open-weight model war narrative dominates Hugging Face discourse: DeepSeek and Qwen challenging Llama and Mistral, with Guillaume Lample discussing Voxtral TTS and Mistral 4 plans in a fresh podcast

people_highlights.name: Mira Murati
people_highlights.post: "Grateful to Jensen and @nvidia team for their support. Together, we're working to deploy at least 1GW of Vera Rubin systems, bringing adaptable collaborative AI to everyone."
people_highlights.engagement: widely reported, significant buzz across tech media
people_highlights.why: Securing 1GW of Nvidia's next-gen Vera Rubin compute is one of the largest infrastructure commitments by a new AI startup — signals Thinking Machines is positioning as a serious frontier-compute player, not just another LLM wrapper

people_highlights.name: Fei-Fei Li
people_highlights.post: "Spatial intelligence will transform how we create and interact with real and virtual worlds — revolutionizing storytelling, creativity, robotics, scientific discovery, and beyond. This is AI's next frontier."
people_highlights.engagement: featured on a16z site, Substack post widely shared
people_highlights.why: A manifesto-level statement from one of AI's most credentialed voices, published with a16z amplification, framing World Labs' spatial intelligence thesis as the post-LLM paradigm shift — likely to shape research and investment narratives

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post: Prompted Gemini 3 with 'show how far AI has come since this post by doing stuff' — the model built a fully interactive, playable 'Candy-Powered FTL Starship Simulator'. Post titled 'Three Years from GPT-3 to Gemini 3': "I am usually pretty hesitant to make technology predictions..."
people_highlights.engagement: high engagement on Substack, widely shared on AI Twitter
people_highlights.why: Mollick's comparative framing — a concrete demo of Gemini 3's capabilities vs. 2022 ChatGPT — gives the broader public an intuitive grasp of AI's 3-year leap, and the 'starship simulator' example is becoming a viral reference point

people_highlights.name: Guillaume Lample
people_highlights.post: Participated in podcast 'Mistral: Voxtral TTS, Forge, Leanstral, & what's next for Mistral 4' discussing new text-to-speech model Voxtral, Forge, Leanstral, and future plans for Mistral 4.
people_highlights.engagement: posted ~3 days ago, no specific metrics available
people_highlights.why: Mistral expanding into TTS with Voxtral alongside Mistral 4 roadmap signals the European open-weight lab is diversifying beyond text — relevant amid intense open-weight competition from Qwen and DeepSeek

tldr: • The week's dominant signal is compute and infrastructure scale: Mira Murati's 1GW Nvidia Vera Rubin deal and Fei-Fei Li's spatial intelligence manifesto both point to a post-LLM-text era taking shape — AI Twitter is reading these as coordinated signals of the next platform shift

• Practitioner sentiment is bifurcating: excitement over Gemini 3's capabilities (Mollick's starship simulator demo) and ElevenLabs v3 voice expressiveness vs. growing frustration with benchmark inflation, vibe-coding tech debt, and unresolved chain-of-thought faithfulness concerns

• The open-weight war is at peak intensity: DeepSeek, Qwen, Mistral (with new TTS and Mistral 4 hints), and Meta's Llama are all in motion — Hugging Face is the gravitational center of this conversation and practitioners are tracking releases daily

• AGI discourse is muddled but loud: Jensen Huang's 'we've achieved AGI' framing and ongoing debates about o3/o4 CoT transparency are keeping existential and definitional questions front-of-mind, even as most practitioners stay focused on shipping real products

briefing_he.community_pulse_he:
• Mira Murati של Thinking Machines הכריזה על שותפות compute בהיקף 1GW עם Nvidia Vera Rubin, מה שנחשב לאחד המהלכים הגדולים ביותר של מייסד לשעבר של OpenAI — הציטוט שלה \״bringing adaptable collaborative AI to everyone\״ מסתובב בחוזקה בקהילה
• Fei-Fei Li פרסמה manifesto על spatial intelligence ב-Substack שהופיע גם ב-a16z, וטוענת ש\״spatial intelligence will transform how we create and interact with real and virtual worlds — revolutionizing storytelling, creativity, robotics, scientific discovery\״
• פוסט של Ethan Mollick בשם \״Three Years from GPT-3 to Gemini 3\״ הפך לווירלי: הוא בקש מ-Gemini 3 להוכיח התקדמות וקיבל simulator אינטראקטיבי מלא של \״Candy-Powered FTL Starship\״ — דוגמה חזקה לקפיצה של AI בשלוש שנים
• כל הדיון על AGI עדיין מתוסבך מטענת Jensen Huang ברדיו עם Lex Fridman: \״I think we\'ve achieved AGI\״ — זה מעניק דלק לוויכוח על timeline של AGI והמחלוקת על הגדרות
• התנגדות ל-\״vibe coding\״ מתגברת: מפתחים מתקיפים את AI agents כמו SWE-agent על כך שהם יוצרים tech debt כשמשתמשים בהם ללא review קפדני — הפריזינג \״beyond the vibes\״ קוזם בקהילות dev
• תחרויות agent frameworks מתלהטות: LangGraph מול CrewAI מול AutoGen הם בכותרות dev.to ו-LinkedIn, וההסכמה הולכת לכיוון graph-based state machines בתור הדרך המבשלת
• ElevenLabs הציגה Eleven v3 ויוצרת buzz סביב real-time voice AI agents עם ביטוי רגשי (נשימה כבדה, לחישה, צחוק) בחביון שמתחת ל-150ms — מעשה קטיגוריה לתוצר voice
• משבר אמון בבenchmarks מעמיק: SWE-bench contamination וגדילת GitHub data leakage עוד בדיון, וקוראות לפרוש את MMLU ו-GPQA — הפריזינג \״benchmarks are not trustworthy\״ הפך ווירלי
• סיפור מלחמת open-weight models שולט ב-Hugging Face: DeepSeek ו-Qwen מאתגרים את Llama ו-Mistral, Guillaume Lample דיבר על Voxtral TTS ותוכניות Mistral 4 בפודקאסט חדש
tldr_he: • הסיגנל החזק של השבוע הוא סקאלה של compute וinfrastructure: ההסכם של Mira Murati על 1GW Vera Rubin וה-manifesto של Fei-Fei Li על spatial intelligence מצביעים על עידן post-LLM שקורה — AI Twitter רואה את זה כאותות מתואמים של shift platformי הבא

• הרגש של המעשיים מתחלק: התרגשות מ-Gemini 3 (ה-starship demo של Mollick) וה-expressiveness של ElevenLabs v3 מול תסכול גדל מ-benchmark inflation, tech debt של vibe-coding, ועניינים לא פתורים בfaithfulness של chain-of-thought
• מלחמת open-weight בחום: DeepSeek, Qwen, Mistral (עם TTS חדש ורמזים ל-Mistral 4), ו-Meta Llama כולם בתנועה — Hugging Face הוא הגרביטציוני של שיחה זו ומפתחים עוקבים אחרי releases כל יום
• דיון AGI מבולבל אבל חזק: הפריזינג של Jensen Huang \״we\'ve achieved AGI\״ וויכוחים על o3/o4 CoT transparency מפעילים שאלות קיומיות והגדרתיות, גם כשרוב המעשיים מתמקדים בביצוע מוצרים אמיתיים
